title: Pay and deductions
text:
Workers should receive payslips and be paid correctly. Different workers have different rights to minimum wage and sick pay.
Employers can only take money out of a worker's pay in limited circumstances.
Advice
National Minimum Wage
What the current minimum wage rates are. What to do if a worker is underpaid.
Sick pay
How much an employer must pay a worker who is off sick.
Payslips
Who gets a payslip and what it must show.
If wages are not paid
How to manage unpaid wages. Checking wages. If the employer is no longer trading.
Deductions from pay and wages
What an employer can take out of a worker's pay. How to handle overpayments and training costs.
Final pay when someone leaves a job
Why a worker's pay might be different when they leave a job. How holiday affects final pay.
Pay for working extra hours
How much employers should pay workers for overtime.
Getting paid for mandatory training
How much employers should pay workers for training they need for their job.
Pay rises
What the different types of pay rises are. Asking for them. What to do if they're not paid.
Bonuses
What the different types of bonuses are. Who is entitled to them. What to do if they're not paid.
Commission
What commission is and how it affects minimum wage. When employment ends or commission is not paid.
Expenses
When expenses should be paid. What to do if they're not.
Tips and service charges
What the law on tips, gratuities and service charges says. What to do if tips are not paid.
Workplace pensions
How workplace pension schemes work. Opting in. Resolving problems.
If you like, you can tell us more about what was useful on this page. We cannot reply – so do not include any personal details, for example your email address or phone number. If you have any questions about your individual circumstances, you can contact the Acas helpline .
